body.title: [gated] Tester rackmount — the first dev-pipeline station; owns the testing container
body.description:
**GATED — north-star tier (D-dev-pipeline-stations-2026-07-07); build when debt-clearing done (offload-loop scale + Windows aider) and Akien reopens.**

**Problem / shape:** The post-build checklist is a PIPELINE of independent station-components; TESTING is the first station to formalize. Today testing is ad-hoc (CC spins subagents / test-fix skill; aider's runner shells pytest IN ITS CLONE ON THE HOST — the builder grading itself). Build a TESTER rackmount (device.py/shim.py) that: (1) takes a built branch/diff, (2) runs its tests in ISOLATION, (3) returns a verdict — an INDEPENDENT grader (separation of powers: the builder never grades its own work).

**Owns the testing container (ABSORBS D-containers-sandbox-device-2026-07-07):** the sandbox container is the tester's execution environment, NOT a standalone device. Untrusted model-generated code runs INSIDE the tester's container — off the host, isolated (imports + FS + net + DB), CP6. Windows-portability + cold-start tradeoffs from the containers decision carry here (fast in-process import-isolation may stay the hot path for cheap runs; container for untrusted/heavy).

**Pipeline implication:** aider's runner STOPS running the gate itself and HANDS OFF to the tester. Build produces the diff; tester validates. This is the separation-of-powers refactor.

**Affected files (at gate-open):** new unseen_university/devices/tester/; aider runner._run_tests / device gate re-routed to dispatch the tester; reconcile emit_merge_proof to consume the tester verdict.

**Completion test:** the tester validates an aider-built branch in its container and returns a verdict; a deliberately-hollow build earns a RED verdict the builder could not have graded away; aider's runner no longer runs the gate on the host.

**Scope boundary:** the tester station + its container. NOT the examiner/merger stations (separate). NOT the full pipeline orchestration decomposition.

**Design rules:** separation of powers (builder != grader); device.py/shim.py design center; container owned by the tester; no SQLITE; Windows-portable.

**Test plan:** the hollow-build-earns-red + isolated-verdict test above.

**intention:** I intend that an independent tester rackmount grades built code in its own sandboxed container, so the builder never certifies itself and untrusted code never runs on the host.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---
**NEW EVIDENCE BEARING ON THE GATE — 2026-07-13. The harm this ticket prevents is now MEASURED, not
hypothetical. The gate remains Akien's to open; this is the case for opening it.**

This ticket says: *"aider's runner shells pytest IN ITS CLONE ON THE HOST — the builder grading itself."*
**Today CC did exactly that, nine times.** Nine `pytest tests/` runs shelled on the host, each driving live
24B inference at Hex's SINGLE inference slot (`-np 1`), each blocking for hours, none terminating, none
cleaning up. They saturated the slot. The resulting queue produced **two confidently-wrong diagnoses** and
**two false artifacts in the memory store** (one of them a ticket *id*) before the load was traced back to
us. See T-default-suite-drives-live-inference-and-saturates-hex (closed proven) and
[[feedback_account_for_your_own_load]].

**THE CONTAINER IS NOT JUST ISOLATION — IT IS THE ADMISSION POINT. This is the load-bearing discovery.**
`unseen_university/devices/skeleton/container_shim.py` (`ContainerShim`) ALREADY EXISTS, and its network
model is **`--network=none` + a Unix domain socket**: *"The container gets no TCP stack at all"*; bridge
networking is explicitly rejected. **A test inside that container CANNOT open a socket to Hex.** Its only
path to inference is UDS -> rack -> the inference device.

So the tester's container and the capacity lease
(T-constrained-resources-need-an-owner-with-admission-control) are **not two independent fixes** — the
container is what makes the lease **UNBYPASSABLE**. A policy ("run tests in series", "don't hammer Hex")
binds only the consumers who read it. `--network=none` binds ALL of them, including the ones nobody
remembered to tell. Contamination and saturation stop being FORBIDDEN and become IMPOSSIBLE.

**GATE DEPENDENCY STATUS:**
  * *"the inference router redesign ships"* — **SHIPPED.** The last dozen commits on
    `devices/inference/` are the escalation POLICY object, typed no-path (NoCapableModel vs
    NoAvailableProvider), the tier ladder made real, and cost-first sort.
  * *"debt-clearing done (offload-loop scale + Windows aider)"* — **NOT verified by CC. Akien's call.**
  * *"and Akien reopens"* — **his gate, not mine. Surfaced, not opened.**

**DO NOT CONFUSE THIS WITH `devices/rack_test/`.** That is `RackTestDevice`/`RackTestShim` — a test DOUBLE
(an instrumented fake BaseDevice with call recording and failure injection, from the CLOSED
T-rackmount-test-object). It runs nothing, contains no container, and is NOT this device. Same neighbourhood,
different animal. CC conflated the two on 2026-07-13 and wrongly argued against building this one.
body.gate: debt-clearing (offload-loop scale + Windows aider) AND Akien reopens
